rationale: plan-approve passes: `mindspec validate plan` exits 0 (sole issue is the advisory decomposition-scope-redundancy R=0.06 WARN — the intentional disjoint cut). Frontmatter schema valid; all four citations exist on disk and every Domain line intersects {core,workflow} (verified: 0037 workflow/execution; 0035 workflow/execution/core; 0034 workflow; 0039 core/workflow/execution/context-system). Coverage holds: core←0035,0039; workflow←all four. Per-bead complete gates walk clean: B1 (.mindspec/adr/** only) is a GENUINE vacuous pass — isDocFile classifies adr/** as doc, so doc-sync early-returns at sourceChanges==0 (after validateSpecArtifactSync no-ops, no spec.md touched) and divergence skips them via isProcessArtifact→isDocFile before attribution; plan.md:185's grep-.go-non-zero bullet is accurate. OWNERSHIP confirms core=internal/config/**, workflow=internal/panel/**+cmd/**, so B2/B3/B4 source attributes to a domain the cited set covers and each carries its matching domain doc (B3 architecture.md, B4 interfaces.md — distinct). depends_on encodes B1 before every source bead (2,3←1; 4←2); gate.go:257 `threshold > 0` third-defense present, panel imports 0 config (leaf holds). Unclaimed-pause staleness is minimal (see findings).
findings: VALIDATOR: `mindspec validate plan 109` exits 0; only WARN is decomposition-scope-redundancy R=0.06 (<0.15) — advisory, non-blocking, and the plan owns it explicitly (package-disjoint 2/3 pair, parallelism ratio 0.25 at the floor). Gate-forward: passes.

B1 VACUOUS PASS CONFIRMED in this tree: isDocFile treats .mindspec/adr/** as doc (lifecycleDocSubtrees includes 'adr'); ValidateDocsRange returns at line 112 with zero sourceChanges; ValidateDivergence's isProcessArtifact→isDocFile drops both ADR files before attribution — no adr-divergence-unowned/uncovered. Not a smuggled source change; genuinely doc-carrying.

B2/B3/B4 ATTRIBUTION CLEAN: OWNERSHIP core claims internal/config/**, workflow claims internal/panel/** and cmd/**. B2 config.go→core covered by cited 0035/0039 + carries core/interfaces.md; B3 panel.go/gate.go→workflow + carries workflow/architecture.md; B4 cmd/mindspec/config.go→workflow (cmd/**) + carries workflow/interfaces.md (a different workflow doc than B3, satisfying internal-docs via hasArtifactPrefix). All divergence-covered by the cited set independent of ADR-0040.

ADR-0040 DEPENDENCY ENCODED: frontmatter depends_on is 1←(none), 2←1, 3←1, 4←2, so ADR-0040 lands before any source bead. Verified ADR-0040 does NOT exist on disk yet, so its correct OMISSION from adr_citations avoids adr-cite-missing. B4←B2 is a real compile-time edge (renderConfig reads the config.Panel/Models/Loop/Runner fields B2 adds).

MINOR (non-blocking): plan.md:100 calls ADR-0040 'an additional core, workflow coverage source' for the later beads — but the divergence gate reads only fm.ADRCitations, which does NOT list ADR-0040, so an on-disk-but-uncited ADR-0040 is NOT a coverage source for this plan's gate. The load-bearing coverage (0035/0039 for core, all four for workflow) is correct and the gate passes regardless, so this is prose imprecision, not a defect. Optional tighten.

UNCLAIMED-PAUSE STALENESS — LOW HAZARD: bd is a durable store and unclaimed-ready is a first-class state; the four spec approve/complete gates are in-binary and re-anchor to each bead's own fork-point (base..head), and fresh-context enforcement means each bead resumes clean regardless of pause length. The depends_on edges become bd dependency edges, so `bd ready` surfaces ONLY B1 until it closes — this mechanically pins the ADR-0040-first ordering even in a cold fresh session, a robustness point in the plan's favor. Only real consideration: if main advances materially before implementation the spec branch needs a re-merge-forward (standard mindspec practice, not a plan defect); the cited ADRs are stable Accepted ADRs so citation drift risk is negligible.

gate.go:257 `if f.Res.Approves >= threshold && threshold > 0` present; ApproveThreshold at panel.go:76 is the sole home (currently N-1, non-positive ExpectedReviewers→0). B3's grep-'threshold > 0' pin and leaf-invariant (`go list -deps ./internal/panel | grep -q internal/config` non-zero, currently 0 config deps) are both satisfiable and correctly encoded.
